conventions.time_unit: milliseconds for every field ending in _ms
conventions.gain_definition: gain = unfused_time / fused_time  (>1.0 => fusion FASTER), matches estimator '1.020x'
conventions.dtype: bfloat16 for all measured tensors; fp32 accumulate
conventions.hand_triton_numerics: judged vs an fp32 reference: ok iff rel_max_vs_fp32 <= max(2 x eager path's own rel_max_vs_fp32, 5e-2). The hand kernel keeps gate/up in fp32 accumulators (MORE accurate than eager, which rounds gu to bf16 pre-silu), so allclose-vs-eager is the wrong criterion at large k
conventions.gemm_drift_ratio: bare-GEMM time re-measured at the END of the config / at the START; bounds DVFS+thermal drift across the config's timed region (~1.0 = clean)
conventions.fused_verified: True iff a Triton GEMM template folds the epilogue in AND no separate silu/mul/add elementwise kernel remains (raw kernel_evidence stored for re-judge)
conventions.compiled_paths: compiled_ms = plain max-autotune (cudagraphs ON; is_big_gpu()<68 SMs so inductor keeps the vendor GEMM + separate epilogue kernel on this GPU); unfused_compiled_nocg_ms / compiled_nocg_ms = max-autotune-no-cudagraphs unforced (for swiglu this is the BEST UNFUSED realization: vendor GEMM + one fused pointwise kernel, exactly the estimator's unfused model); compiled_forced_triton_ms = is_big_gpu patched + TRITON-only autotune backends + no-cudagraphs -> a genuine Triton GEMM template with the epilogue folded in (the object the task asks about); fused_verified_forced judges it
conventions.measured_gain_verified: best_unfused_ms / best VERIFIED-fused path (hand triton by construction; forced template iff fused_verified_forced; addmm iff addmm_fused_verified; compiled paths iff template- or vendor-fused per evidence). The A-vs-B verdict keys on THIS, not measured_gain
conventions.addmm_fused_verified: residual only: torch.addmm profile shows no separate add/elementwise kernel (Memcpy DtoD tolerated: beta-accumulate copy)
conventions.compiled_cudagraph_input_copy_us: GPU time of multi_tensor_apply static-input-copy kernels inside ONE compiled call (cudagraph deployment overhead, not fusion work); subtract from compiled_ms for a kernels-only fused-vs-unfused comparison
conventions.timing: median of 30 cuda-event samples, 15 warmup, per config

env.clocks_note: WSL2 / no root: clocks NOT locked -> ClockSampler recorded per config

annotations_post_hoc.note: Added after the run during analysis/review (2026-07-21). residual_numerics uses FRESH seed-0 tensors at the same dims (not the run's exact tensors): the strict allclose numerics_ok=false flags on residual rows are false alarms -- addmm/fused paths round once where eager mm+add rounds twice; values differ by <=1 bf16 ulp and the fused paths are closer to the fp32 reference than eager is. derived_late_phase gives drift-robust within-late-phase gains (both operands measured after the compile/autotune phase) and *_over_gemm_repeat re-references fused kernels to the CONTEMPORANEOUS end-of-config GEMM. swiglu_M2048_h1024 is flagged baseline_unmeasurable (unfused_ms < gemm_only_ms, impossible at steady clock).

clean5_plus_positive_drift_tainted_n7.note: clean5 + the two drift-excluded rows that carried POSITIVE verified gains (swiglu_M2048_h2048 drift 0.87, residual_M2048 drift 1.14); the honest "include the tainted-but-plausible evidence" sensitivity

aggregates.nonthrottled_n7_note: CAUTION: this set includes swiglu_M2048_h1024 (baseline unmeasurable, mv 0.879) — it is the drift<1.05 cut, not a usable-evidence cut
